x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces MOCJ's scattered content (programs, publications, the single Supervised Release dataset, and the system indicators it analyzes) and adds refer_to_program — the net-new write workflow a coordination office needs, which today exists only offline or behind a generic Contact Form 7 web form.

server.name: io.nyc.mocj
server.title: NYC Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice (MOCJ)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Mayor's Office of Criminal Justice — its coordinated programs (by issue and Sequential Intercept stage), publications and data reports, procurement solicitations, the single Supervised Release Dockets dataset, and the aggregate jail-population / system indicators it analyzes — plus the net-new ability to make and track a program referral, which today happens only offline or via a generic web form.
server.instructions: MOCJ is a policy, convening, and grant-making office, so most of its 'data' is narrative: use find_programs / get_program to locate MOCJ-coordinated programs by borough, issue area, or Sequential Intercept stage. find_supervised_release_dockets queries the ONE MOCJ Open Data dataset (court dockets released to Supervised Release). find_jail_population_metrics returns AGGREGATE system indicators (jail population, admissions, re-arrest) — note MOCJ mostly analyzes and links to numbers OWNED BY other agencies (DOC, NYPD, DCJS, BOC), it rarely owns them. find_data_reports covers reports, briefs, fact sheets, and data stories (mostly PDF explainers). find_solicitations covers procurement. refer_to_program (net-new) routes a person or case to a program; it mutates state and handles SENSITIVE justice-involved data — require an authenticated referrer, confirm the program, need, and that consent was given before submitting, and never include unnecessary personal identifiers.

tools.name: find_programs
tools.title: Find Programs
tools.description: Search MOCJ-coordinated community programs by borough, issue area, Sequential Intercept stage, program type, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_program
tools.title: Get Program
tools.description: Fetch a MOCJ program (provider, issue areas, Sequential Intercept stage, eligibility) by id.

tools.name: find_supervised_release_dockets
tools.title: Find Supervised Release Dockets
tools.description: Search the single MOCJ Open Data dataset — court dockets released into the Supervised Release program — by supervising organization, supervision level, or entry date.

tools.name: find_jail_population_metrics
tools.title: Find Jail Population Metrics
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE jail-population and system indicators MOCJ analyzes (daily population, admissions, discharges, arrests, re-arrest rate). Note MOCJ mostly links to numbers owned by DOC/NYPD/DCJS/BOC; this surfaces the metrics from its explainers.

tools.name: find_data_reports
tools.title: Find Data Reports & Publications
tools.description: Search MOCJ publications — reports, briefs, fact sheets, scorecards, data reports, and data stories (mostly PDF explainers, e.g. jail-population increase and re-arrest analyses).

tools.name: find_solicitations
tools.title: Find Solicitations
tools.description: List MOCJ procurement solicitations and notices — how the office contracts with grantee providers — by status, kind, or issue area.

tools.name: get_solicitation
tools.title: Get Solicitation
tools.description: Fetch a MOCJ procurement solicitation or notice by id.

tools.name: refer_to_program
tools.title: Refer to a Program
tools.description: Make a MOCJ program referral — route a person or court case to a coordinated program. NET-NEW workflow with no public API today; referrals happen offline or via a generic Contact Form 7 web form. Requires an authenticated referrer. Mutates state and handles SENSITIVE justice-involved data — confirm the program, need, and that consent was given first; set urgent=true for time-sensitive referrals; never include unnecessary personal identifiers.

tools.name: get_referral
tools.title: Get Referral
tools.description: Fetch a program referral and its status. Requires an authenticated referrer; returns sensitive data.
